statement.en: The CriticalSet problem: Identifying Critical Contributors in Bipartite Dependency Networks
statement.summary: Identifying critical nodes in complex networks is a fundamental task in graph mining. Yet, methods addressing an all-or-nothing coverage mechanics in a bipartite dependency network, a graph with two types of nodes where edges represent dependency relationships across the two groups only, remain largely unexplored. We formalize the CriticalSet problem: given an arbitrary bipartite graph modeling dependencies of items on contributors, identify the set of k contributors whose removal isolates the largest number of items. We prove that this problem is NP-hard and requires maximizing a supermodular set function, for which standard forward greedy algorithms provide no approximation guarantees. Consequently, we model CriticalSet as a coalitional game, deriving a closed-form centrality, ShapleyCov

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom proposes a formally defined NP-hard combinatorial optimization problem (CriticalSet) with proven computational hardness. NP-hardness is a theoretical limit classification—the problem is decidable but computationally intractable for general instances. The use of supermodular set functions and game-theoretic (Shapley value) approximations situates this in the INFINITY category: well-defined, mathematically sound, but subject to inherent computational bounds.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 貢献者の価値は、彼らが加わることで隔離される項目の周辺利得の平均で測られる。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 左の寄与者を削除すると、右の項目が孤立する度合いが、ネットワークの脆弱性を測る。
